title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat, but its usage could lead to potential risks if improperly configured or if malicious actors exploit its functionality to identify vulnerabilities in target systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment before deploying it in production systems.
Ensure proper access controls and logging are in place to monitor its usage and prevent unauthorized access.
Educate users on the responsible use of vulnerability scanning tools to avoid misuse or accidental exposure of sensitive information.

title: Apache Airflow versions 3.1.0 through 3.1.7 missing authorization vulnerability in the Execution...
description:
Apache Airflow versions 3.1.0 through 3.1.7 missing authorization vulnerability in the Execution API's Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) endpoints that allows any authenticated task instance to read, approve, or reject HITL workflows belonging to any other task instance.


Users are recommended to upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.1.8 or later, which resolves this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-30911.

title: A flaw was found in libsoup, a library for handling HTTP requests. This vulnerability, known as a...
description: A flaw was found in libsoup, a library for handling HTTP requests. This vulnerability, known as a Use-After-Free, occurs in the HTTP/2 server implementation. A remote attacker can exploit this by sending specially crafted HTTP/2 requests that cause authentication failures. This can lead to the application attempting to access memory that has already been freed, potentially causing application instability or crashes, resulting in a Denial of Service (DoS).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4271.

title: Apache Airflow versions 3.1.0 through 3.1.7 session token (_token) in cookies is set to path=/...
description:
Apache Airflow versions 3.1.0 through 3.1.7 session token (_token) in cookies is set to path=/ regardless of the configured [webserver] base_url or [api] base_url.
This allows any application co-hosted under the same domain to capture valid Airflow session tokens from HTTP request headers, allowing full session takeover without attacking Airflow itself.

Users are recommended to upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.1.8 or later, which resolves this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows co-hosted applications on the same domain to capture valid Apache Airflow session tokens, leading to potential full session takeovers. This could result in unauthorized access to sensitive workflows, data exfiltration, or malicious manipulation of Airflow processes.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to Apache Airflow version 3.1.8 or later to address the vulnerability.
Audit web server configurations to ensure that no untrusted applications are co-hosted under the same domain as Apache Airflow.
Implement additional security controls, such as domain isolation and HTTP header protections, to mitigate risks from similar vulnerabilities.

title: HCL Sametime is vulnerable to broken server-side validation. While the application performs...
description: HCL Sametime is vulnerable to broken server-side validation. While the application performs client-side input checks, these are not enforced by the web server. An attacker can bypass these restrictions by sending manipulated HTTP requests directly to the server.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2025-31966.

title: Apache Airflow versions 3.1.0 through 3.1.7 /ui/dependencies endpoint returns the full DAG...
description:
Apache Airflow versions 3.1.0 through 3.1.7 /ui/dependencies endpoint returns the full DAG dependency graph without filtering by authorized DAG IDs. This allows an authenticated user with only DAG Dependencies permission to enumerate DAGs they are not authorized to view.


Users are recommended to upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.1.8 or later, which resolves this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28563.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

audit: add fchmodat2() to...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

audit: add fchmodat2() to change attributes class

fchmodat2(), introduced in version 6.6 is currently not in the change
attribute class of audit. Calling fchmodat2() to change a file
attribute in the same fashion than chmod() or fchmodat() will bypass
audit rules such as:

-w /tmp/test -p rwa -k test_rwa

The current patch adds fchmodat2() to the change attributes class.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel allows the fchmodat2() system call to bypass audit rules, potentially enabling unauthorized file attribute changes without detection. This could lead to unauthorized modifications, privilege escalation, or data tampering.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest Linux kernel version that includes the patch for CVE-2025-71239.
Review and update audit rules to ensure proper monitoring of file attribute changes, including calls to fchmodat2().
Conduct a security review of systems using Linux kernel 6.6 or later to identify potential misuse of the fchmodat2() system call.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

audit: add missing syscalls...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

audit: add missing syscalls to read class

The "at" variant of getxattr() and listxattr() are missing from the
audit read class. Calling getxattrat() or listxattrat() on a file to
read its extended attributes will bypass audit rules such as:

-w /tmp/test -p rwa -k test_rwa

The current patch adds missing syscalls to the audit read class.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows certain syscalls to bypass audit rules, potentially enabling unauthorized access to sensitive file attributes without detection. This could lead to data leakage or unauthorized activity monitoring evasion.
recommended_actions: Update Linux kernel to the latest version that includes the patch for CVE-2026-23241.
Review and enhance audit rules to ensure proper monitoring of file attribute access.
Conduct a security assessment to identify potential exploitation of this vulnerability in your environment.

title: Apache Airflow versions 3.0.0 through 3.1.7 FastAPI DagVersion listing API does not apply per-DAG...
description:
Apache Airflow versions 3.0.0 through 3.1.7 FastAPI DagVersion listing API does not apply per-DAG authorization filtering when the request is made with dag_id set to "~" (wildcard for all DAGs). As a result, version metadata of DAGs that the requester is not authorized to access is returned.


Users are recommended to upgrade to Apache Airflow 3.1.8 or later, which resolves this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-26929.

title: A flaw was found in libsoup. An attacker controlling the value used to set the Content-Type...
description: A flaw was found in libsoup. An attacker controlling the value used to set the Content-Type header can inject a Carriage Return Line Feed (CRLF) sequence due to improper input sanitization in the `soup_message_headers_set_content_type()` function. This vulnerability allows for the injection of arbitrary header-value pairs, potentially leading to HTTP header injection and response splitting attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3634.

title: A flaw was found in libsoup, a library used by applications to send network requests. This...
description: A flaw was found in libsoup, a library used by applications to send network requests. This vulnerability occurs because libsoup does not properly validate hostnames, allowing special characters to be injected into HTTP headers. A remote attacker could exploit this to perform HTTP smuggling, where they can send hidden, malicious requests alongside legitimate ones. In certain situations, this could lead to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), enabling an attacker to force the server to make unautho

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in libsoup could allow attackers to exploit HTTP smuggling, potentially leading to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). This may enable unauthorized access to internal systems or sensitive data, depending on the server's configuration and the applications using the library.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of libsoup that addresses CVE-2026-3632, as soon as a patch is available.
Implement strict input validation and sanitization in applications using libsoup to mitigate potential exploitation.
Monitor network traffic for unusual patterns or signs of HTTP smuggling attacks and review server configurations to minimize SSRF risks.

title: A flaw was found in libsoup. A remote attacker, by controlling the method parameter of the ...
description: A flaw was found in libsoup. A remote attacker, by controlling the method parameter of the `soup_message_new()` function, could inject arbitrary headers and additional request data. This vulnerability, known as CRLF (Carriage Return Line Feed) injection, occurs because the method value is not properly escaped during request line construction, potentially leading to HTTP request injection.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in libsoup could allow remote attackers to inject arbitrary headers and request data, potentially leading to HTTP request smuggling or manipulation of server behavior. However, the severity is low as exploitation requires specific conditions and the flaw is limited in scope.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest version of libsoup as soon as a patch is available to address CVE-2026-3633.
Implement input validation and sanitization for any user-controlled parameters in applications using libsoup.
Monitor application logs for unusual HTTP request patterns that could indicate exploitation attempts.

title: The extension fails to properly reset the generated MFA code after successful authentication....
description: The extension fails to properly reset the generated MFA code after successful authentication. This leads to a possible MFA bypass for future login attempts by providing an empty string as MFA code to the extensions MFA provider.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4208.

title: The extension fails to verify, if an authenticated user has permissions to access to redirects...
description: The extension fails to verify, if an authenticated user has permissions to access to redirects resulting in exposure of redirect records when editing a page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4202.

title: Missing Authorization vulnerability in Pluggabl Booster for WooCommerce allows Exploiting...
description: Missing Authorization vulnerability in Pluggabl Booster for WooCommerce allows Exploiting Incorrectly Configured Access Control Security Levels.This issue affects Booster for WooCommerce: from n/a before 7.11.3.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The Missing Authorization vulnerability in Booster for WooCommerce could allow unauthorized users to exploit incorrectly configured access control settings, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or manipulation of sensitive e-commerce data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Booster for WooCommerce to version 7.11.3 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Audit and review access control configurations to ensure proper security settings are applied.
Monitor for any unusual activity or unauthorized access attempts in your WooCommerce environment.

title: GCB/FCB Audit Software developed by DrangSoft has a Missing Authentication vulnerability,...
description: GCB/FCB Audit Software developed by DrangSoft has a Missing Authentication vulnerability, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to directly access certain APIs to create a new administrative account.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4312.

title: In affected versions of Octopus Server it was possible for a low privileged user to manipulate an...
description: In affected versions of Octopus Server it was possible for a low privileged user to manipulate an API request to change the signing key expiration and revocation time frames via an API endpoint that had incorrect permission validation. It was not possible to expose the signing keys using this vulnerability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-3237.

title: A weakness has been identified in frdel/agent0ai agent-zero 0.9.7. This affects the function...
description: A weakness has been identified in frdel/agent0ai agent-zero 0.9.7. This affects the function handle_pdf_document of the file python/helpers/document_query.py. This manipulation causes server-side request forgery. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The identified vulnerability in the handle_pdf_document function of agent-zero 0.9.7 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), enabling attackers to manipulate server requests. This could lead to unauthorized access to internal systems, data exfiltration, or further exploitation of the affected infrastructure. The public availability of the exploit increases the likelihood of attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization to the handle_pdf_document function to mitigate SSRF risks.
Monitor and block suspicious outbound traffic from the affected server to detect and prevent potential exploitation attempts.
Contact the vendor again and escalate the issue while considering alternative solutions or patches if the vendor remains unresponsive.

title: All versions of the package sjcl are vulnerable to Improper Verification of Cryptographic...
description: All versions of the package sjcl are vulnerable to Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature due to missing point-on-curve validation in sjcl.ecc.basicKey.publicKey(). An attacker can recover a victim's ECDH private key by sending crafted off-curve public keys and observing ECDH outputs. The dhJavaEc() function directly returns the raw x-coordinate of the scalar multiplication result (no hashing), providing a plaintext oracle without requiring any decryption feedback.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4258.

title: The Royal Addons for Elementor – Addons and Templates Kit for Elementor plugin for WordPress is...
description: The Royal Addons for Elementor – Addons and Templates Kit for Elementor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Information Exposure in all versions up to, and including, 1.7.1049 via the get_main_query_args() function due to insufficient restrictions on which posts can be included. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to extract contents of non-public custom post types, such as Contact Form 7 submissions or WooCommerce coupons.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Royal Addons for Elementor plugin allows unauthenticated attackers to access sensitive information from non-public custom post types. This could lead to data leakage, exposing sensitive data such as customer information, form submissions, or discount codes, potentially resulting in reputational damage, financial loss, or compliance violations.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Royal Addons for Elementor plugin to a patched version if available, or disable the plugin temporarily if no fix is provided.
Restrict access to sensitive data by implementing additional access controls and monitoring for unauthorized access attempts.
Review server logs for any signs of exploitation and secure any exposed data, such as resetting compromised credentials or invalidating leaked coupons.

title: A security flaw has been discovered in frdel/agent0ai agent-zero 0.9.7-10. The impacted element...
description: A security flaw has been discovered in frdel/agent0ai agent-zero 0.9.7-10. The impacted element is the function get_abs_path of the file python/helpers/files.py. The manipulation results in path traversal. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform path traversal attacks, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive files and data. This could compromise system integrity and confidentiality.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization to the get_abs_path function in python/helpers/files.py to prevent path traversal.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and unauthorized access, especially targeting file paths.
Contact the vendor again and escalate the issue to ensure a patch is developed and released promptly.

title: CVE-2026-28693 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, an integer overflow in DIB coder can result in out of bounds read or write. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, MAT decoder uses 32-bit arithmetic due to incorrect parenthesization resulting in a heap over-read. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, an uninitialized pointer dereference vulnerability exists in the JBIG decoder due to a missing check. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

title: CVE-2026-28690 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, a stack buffer overflow vulnerability exists in the MNG encoder. There is a bounds checks missing that could corrupting the stack with attacker-controlled data. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28690.

title: CVE-2026-28689 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, domain="path" authorization is checked before final file open/use. A symlink swap between check-time and use-time bypasses policy-denied read/write. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28689.

title: CVE-2026-28688 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, a heap-use-after-free vulnerability exists in the MSL encoder, where a cloned image is destroyed twice. The MSL coder does not support writing MSL so the write capability has been removed. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28688.

title: CVE-2026-28687 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, a heap use-after-free vulnerability in ImageMagick's MSL decoder allows an attacker to trigger access to freed memory by crafting an MSL file. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28687.

title: CVE-2026-28686 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, A heap-buffer-overflow vulnerability exists in the PCL encode due to an undersized output buffer allocation. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28686.

title: CVE-2026-28494 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41, a stack buffer overflow exists in ImageMagick's morphology kernel parsing functions. User-controlled kernel strings exceeding a buffer are copied into fixed-size stack buffers via memcpy without bounds checking, resulting in stack corruption. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16 and 6.9.13-41.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28494.

title: CVE-2026-28493 - ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior
description: ImageMagick is free and open-source software used for editing and manipulating digital images. Prior to versions 7.1.2-16, an integer overflow vulnerability exists in the SIXEL decoer. The vulnerability allows an attacker to perform an out of bounds via a specially crafted image. This vulnerability is fixed in 7.1.2-16.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28493.

title: CVE-2026-28433 - Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers running versions 10.
description: Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers running versions 10.93.0 and later, but prior to 2026.3.1, contain a vulnerability that allows importing other users' data due to lack of ownership validation. The impact of this vulnerability is estimated to be relatively low, as bad actors would require the ID corresponding to the target file for import. This vulnerability is fixed in 2026.3.1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28433.

title: CVE-2026-28432 - Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers prior to 2026.3.1 co
description: Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers prior to 2026.3.1 contain a vulnerability that allows bypassing HTTP signature verification. Although this is a vulnerability related to federation, it affects all servers regardless of whether federation is enabled or disabled. This vulnerability is fixed in 2026.3.1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28432.

title: CVE-2026-28431 - Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers running versions 8.4
description: Misskey is an open source, federated social media platform. All Misskey servers running versions 8.45.0 and later, but prior to 2026.3.1, contain a vulnerability that allows bad actors access to data that they ordinarily wouldn't be able to access due to insufficient permission checks and proper input validation. This vulnerability occurs regardless of whether federation is enabled or not. This vulnerability could lead to a significant data breach. This vulnerability is fixed in 2026.3.1.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28431.

title: CVE-2026-26982 - Ghostty is a cross-platform terminal emulator. Ghostty allows control characters such as 0x03 (Ctrl+
description: Ghostty is a cross-platform terminal emulator. Ghostty allows control characters such as 0x03 (Ctrl+C) in pasted and dropped text. These can be used to execute arbitrary commands in some shell environments. This attack requires an attacker to convince the user to copy and paste or drag and drop malicious text. The attack requires user interaction to be triggered, but the dangerous characters are invisible in most GUI environments so it isn't trivially detected, especially if the string contents are complex. Fixed in Ghostty v1.3.0.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-26982.

title: CVE-2026-1776 - Camaleon CMS versions 2.4.5.0 through 2.9.0, prior to commit f54a77e, contain a path traversal vulne
description: Camaleon CMS versions 2.4.5.0 through 2.9.0, prior to commit f54a77e, contain a path traversal vulnerability in the AWS S3 uploader implementation that allows authenticated users to read arbitrary files from the web server’s filesystem. The issue occurs in the download_private_file functionality when the application is configured to use the CamaleonCmsAwsUploader backend. Unlike the local uploader implementation, the AWS uploader does not validate file paths with valid_folder_path?, allowing directory traversal sequences to be supplied via the file parameter. As a result, any authenticated user, including low-privileged registered users, can access sensitive files such as /etc/passwd. This issue represents a bypass of the incomplete fix for CVE-2024-46987 and affects deployments using the AWS S3 storage backend.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-1776.
